title: 5-Minute Box Breathing Exercise
description: Box breathing: inhale 4 counts, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4. Repeat 5 cycles. Used by Navy SEALs and elite athletes to rapidly calm the nervous system. Activates the parasympathetic response within 90 seconds.

title: Progressive Muscle Relaxation (Full Body)
description: Systematically tense and release each muscle group from toes to forehead. Reduces chronic physical tension stored from stress. Research shows 30-40% reduction in cortisol after a single session.

title: The 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding Technique
description: Name 5 things you see, 4 you can touch, 3 you hear, 2 you smell, 1 you taste. Pulls your mind from spiralling thoughts back to the present moment. Works within 2-3 minutes for most people.

title: 10-Minute Guided Meditation for Anxiety
description: A gentle guided session combining breath awareness and body scan. No experience required. Research shows even single sessions reduce cortisol and self-reported anxiety significantly.

title: Journaling for Difficult Emotions (15 Prompts)
description: 15 CBT-informed prompts to write through sadness, anger, or confusion. Externalising emotions on paper creates psychological distance and measurably reduces their intensity.

title: Yoga for Stress Relief — Beginner Flow
description: A 20-minute gentle flow focusing on hip openers and forward folds — poses that directly activate the parasympathetic nervous system. No prior yoga experience required.

title: Cold Water Face Splash Reset
description: Splash cold water on your face or hold ice cubes for 30 seconds. Activates the dive reflex, slowing heart rate almost immediately. One of the fastest physiological interventions known.

title: Body Scan Meditation (15 Minutes)
description: A guided body scan teaching you to notice physical sensations without judgment. Restores the mind-body connection often severed by chronic stress and anxiety.

title: Loving-Kindness (Metta) Meditation
description: Generate warmth for yourself and others through this ancient Buddhist practice. Clinical trials show measurable increases in positive affect and decreases in self-criticism after 8 weeks.

title: TIPP Skills — Dialectical Behaviour Therapy
description: DBT's TIPP: Temperature change (cold water), Intense exercise, Paced breathing, Paired muscle relaxation. Fast-acting for when emotions feel out of control. Clinically validated.

title: Mindful Walking Meditation
description: Transform a walk into meditation by focusing on each step, breath, and sensory input. Research: 20 minutes in nature drops cortisol by 21%. No equipment needed.

title: 4-7-8 Breathing for Panic Attacks
description: The 4-7-8 technique: inhale 4, hold 7, exhale 8. Activates the vagus nerve and forces a state of calm. Dr Andrew Weil calls it a natural tranquiliser for the nervous system.

title: STOP Mindfulness Technique
description: Stop, Take a breath, Observe your thoughts and feelings, Proceed mindfully. A 60-second pause technique from MBSR that interrupts automatic reactive patterns.

title: Self-Compassion Break (Kristin Neff)
description: Three-step practice: acknowledge suffering, remind yourself suffering is universal, offer yourself kindness. From Dr Kristin Neff's self-compassion research programme.

title: Emotional Freedom Technique (Tapping)
description: Tapping on acupressure meridian points while verbalising distress. Multiple RCTs show significant reductions in anxiety and PTSD symptoms. Unusual but well-evidenced.

title: Cold Shower Exposure Protocol
description: 2-minute cold shower at the end of your regular shower. Research by Dr Andrew Huberman shows 2.5× increase in dopamine lasting 3+ hours. Builds stress resilience over time.

title: Gratitude Three Good Things
description: Write three specific good things that happened today and why they happened. Martin Seligman's research shows this single intervention reduces depression and increases wellbeing significantly.

title: Behavioural Activation for Depression
description: Schedule one meaningful or pleasurable activity per day regardless of mood. CBT's most validated technique for breaking the depression-inactivity cycle.

title: Physiological Sigh (Stanford Protocol)
description: Double inhale through the nose (sniff twice) followed by a long slow exhale. Instantly deflates air sacs in the lungs and resets CO2 levels. Fastest known stress relief technique.

title: Sleep Restriction Therapy (CBT-I)
description: The most evidence-based non-drug insomnia treatment: temporarily restrict sleep to consolidate it, then gradually extend. More effective than sleeping pills long term.

title: Understanding Anxiety: The Science
description: Explains fight-or-flight in plain language — why heart races, hands sweat, mind spirals. Understanding the biology reduces the fear of anxiety itself. Includes the role of the amygdala and HPA axis.

title: How to Stop Ruminating: CBT Guide
description: Breaks the cycle of repetitive negative thinking using cognitive restructuring. Covers thought records, attention training, worry postponement, and scheduled worry time.

title: The Complete Science of Stress
description: How cortisol rewires the brain over time, the difference between acute and chronic stress, and evidence-based strategies to build resilience including sleep, exercise, and social connection.

title: Depression vs Sadness: Knowing the Difference
description: Compassionate guide to clinical depression vs normal sadness. Includes DSM-5 criteria explained accessibly, when to seek help, and what treatment looks like.

title: Setting Healthy Boundaries Without Guilt
description: Why boundaries are acts of love, not selfishness. Includes practical scripts for 8 common difficult boundary conversations. Based on attachment and communication research.

title: Sleep Hygiene: Complete Evidence-Based Guide
description: Everything that actually works for sleep: light exposure, temperature optimisation, consistent timing, caffeine half-life, and which supplements have evidence. No fluff.

title: Cognitive Distortions: 14 Thinking Traps
description: All 14 cognitive distortions (all-or-nothing, catastrophising, mind reading, etc.) with relatable examples and reframing exercises. Foundation of CBT self-help.

title: The Neuroscience of Gratitude
description: How gratitude practice physically changes the medial prefrontal cortex and increases dopamine. Includes a simple 3-minute daily practice backed by neuroimaging research.

title: Loneliness: The Modern Epidemic
description: The loneliness epidemic is as damaging as smoking 15 cigarettes a day per meta-analysis. Evidence-based strategies to build genuine connection and overcome social isolation.

title: Anger Management: Evidence-Based Guide
description: The physiology of rage, healthy vs problematic anger, and the most effective regulation strategies including the pause technique, STOPP method, and anger journaling.

title: Burnout: Signs Causes and Recovery
description: The three dimensions of burnout (exhaustion, cynicism, inefficacy), how to diagnose which phase you're in, and a staged 12-week recovery protocol from HBR research.

title: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy (ACT)
description: How to stop fighting difficult emotions using psychological flexibility. Includes the key ACT processes: defusion, acceptance, values clarification, and committed action.

title: Digital Detox: A 7-Day Protocol
description: Why constant connectivity worsens anxiety and erodes self-esteem. A structured week-by-week plan to reduce screen time and set healthy digital boundaries.

title: Attachment Theory: How It Affects You
description: How your early attachment style (secure, anxious, avoidant, disorganised) shapes adult relationships. Includes assessment questions and pathways to earned security.

title: The Polyvagal Theory Explained Simply
description: Porges's theory of three nervous system states (ventral vagal/safe, sympathetic/fight-flight, dorsal vagal/shutdown) and how to move between them. Revolutionary for trauma.

title: Intrusive Thoughts: What They Mean
description: The science of intrusive thoughts — everyone has them, they don't make you a bad person. Why trying to suppress them backfires and what actually works instead.

title: Grief: The Non-Linear Journey
description: Why the Kübler-Ross stages are misunderstood, what grief actually looks like (non-linear, wave-like), and how to support yourself through loss without forcing a timeline.

title: Social Anxiety: From Avoidance to Connection
description: The difference between shyness and social anxiety disorder, why avoidance makes it worse, and how exposure-based therapy systematically reduces social fear.

title: The Science of Procrastination
description: Why procrastination is an emotional regulation problem, not a time management problem. Research-backed interventions including implementation intentions and temptation bundling.

title: Building Emotional Resilience
description: The seven pillars of resilience (connection, wellness, healthy thinking, meaning, coping, identity) and concrete practices to build each one. Based on APA resilience framework.

title: Headspace: Guided Meditations
description: Hundreds of meditations from 3–20 minutes on stress, anxiety, sleep, and focus. The beginner Take10 series is free and rated as the best meditation app in multiple studies.

title: Andrew Huberman: Stress Control Tools
description: Stanford neuroscientist explains the physiological sigh — the single fastest known stress-relief technique. Plus tools for long-term stress inoculation and the neuroscience behind them.

title: Therapy in a Nutshell: CBT for Anxiety
description: Mental health counsellor Emma McAdam explains CBT techniques for anxiety in a clear, warm, non-clinical style. One of the best free mental health channels on YouTube.

title: DARE Response for Panic and Anxiety
description: Barry McDonagh's DARE response: Defuse, Allow, Run towards, Engage. A counterintuitive approach that ends the anxiety loop by removing the resistance that sustains it.

title: The Happiness Lab with Dr Laurie Santos
description: Yale professor dismantles myths about happiness using cutting-edge psychology. Warm, funny, evidence-based. Covers connection, gratitude, social comparison, and the science of a good life.

title: Wim Hof Breathing Technique
description: Controlled hyperventilation shown to reduce inflammation, elevate mood, and build mental toughness. 11-minute guided session with measurable physiological effects.

title: Yoga with Adriene: Yoga for Anxiety
description: Adriene's most-watched anxiety practice: slow, grounding, deeply calming. 28 million views. Focuses on poses proven to activate the vagal brake.

title: Matt D'Avella: Recovering from Burnout
description: Thoughtful exploration of burnout and what actually helped: doing less, not more, and redefining productivity. Beautifully shot and genuinely moving.

title: NOCD: Understanding OCD and ERP
description: Clear explanation of Exposure and Response Prevention — the gold-standard OCD treatment — by therapists who specialise in it. Separates OCD myths from evidence.

title: How to Process Emotions (Patrick Teahan)
description: Trauma-informed therapist explains how to feel emotions fully without being overwhelmed by them. Especially helpful for those who grew up in homes where emotions were suppressed.

title: Brené Brown: The Power of Vulnerability
description: Brené Brown's legendary TED talk — the most viewed TED talk on psychology. Research on shame, vulnerability, and what it takes to live wholeheartedly.

title: Viktor Frankl: Man's Search for Meaning
description: A documentary on Viktor Frankl's logotherapy — finding meaning even in unavoidable suffering. His work in Auschwitz and the insight that meaning is the primary human motivation.

title: Sleep: The Latest Science (Matthew Walker)
description: UC Berkeley sleep scientist explains what happens to the brain during sleep deprivation and the devastating effects on mental health, immune function, and emotional regulation.

title: Meditations for Loneliness (Tara Brach)
description: Tara Brach's guided meditation specifically for the pain of loneliness — turning towards the feeling rather than fleeing it, and discovering the belonging that's always present.

title: Dr K on Depression: What Actually Helps
description: HealthyGamer psychiatrist Dr Alok Kanojia (Dr K) explains the neuroscience of depression, why willpower doesn't work, and integrative approaches including lifestyle, therapy, and ayurvedic insights.

title: Mel Robbins: The 5-Second Rule
description: Count 5-4-3-2-1 and physically move before your brain talks you out of it. Backed by metacognition research and used by millions to overcome morning paralysis and procrastination.

title: Nightly Wind-Down Yoga (20 min)
description: A gentle 20-minute pre-sleep sequence with legs up the wall, supine twists, and child's pose. Activates the rest-and-digest system and lowers cortisol for better sleep onset.

title: EFT Tapping for Anxiety Relief
description: Follow-along tapping sequence targeting the 8 main acupressure points while voicing your anxiety. Multiple RCTs show cortisol drops 24% in a single session.

title: Anger Release: Physical Workout
description: A high-intensity 20-minute workout specifically designed to discharge anger energy: burpees, shadow boxing, and sprint intervals. Exercise metabolises adrenaline and cortisol.

title: Shame Resilience Practice (Brené Brown)
description: Identify the shame trigger, reality-check it, reach out to a trusted person, speak shame. Brené Brown's four-step shame resilience practice from her research on shame and vulnerability.

title: Dialectical Behaviour Therapy Overview
description: Complete overview of DBT's four skill modules: mindfulness, distress tolerance, emotion regulation, and interpersonal effectiveness. The most comprehensive emotion skills training available.

title: EMDR Therapy: How It Works
description: How Eye Movement Desensitisation and Reprocessing works — bilateral stimulation while recalling traumatic memories. Recommended by WHO for PTSD. Faster than traditional talk therapy.

title: The Sleep-Anxiety-Depression Cycle
description: Why poor sleep worsens anxiety and depression — and how they in turn worsen sleep. How to identify which came first and break the cycle using targeted interventions.

title: Perfectionism vs High Standards
description: The clinical distinction between healthy high standards and maladaptive perfectionism. How perfectionism drives anxiety and procrastination, and CBT interventions that work.

title: Somatic Experiencing for Trauma
description: Peter Levine's body-based approach to trauma — discharging trapped fight-flight energy through physical sensation awareness. How trauma lives in the body and how to release it.

title: How Exercise Treats Depression
description: Why exercise is as effective as antidepressants for mild-moderate depression. The role of BDNF, serotonin, and neuroplasticity. Specific prescriptions: type, intensity, frequency, duration.

title: Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR)
description: Jon Kabat-Zinn's 8-week programme: the most evidence-based mindfulness intervention. What it involves, what the research shows, and how to access it free online.

title: The Freeze Response: Shutdown Explained
description: The least-discussed trauma response: freezing and shutting down. Why it happens, what it feels like (numbness, fog, paralysis), and how to gently activate from freeze.

title: Compassion Fatigue and Caregiver Burnout
description: The hidden cost of caring for others. Signs of compassion fatigue, how it differs from burnout, and a recovery plan for caregivers, therapists, and helpers.

title: Values Clarification Exercise (ACT)
description: Identify your core values — the compass that guides committed action in ACT. Includes the 60-value sort exercise and guidance on setting values-aligned goals when depressed.

title: Crisis Text Line: How It Works
description: Free 24/7 crisis text support for mental health emergencies. How it works, what to expect, and when to use it. Text HOME to 741741 in the US.

title: Emotional Regulation Skills (DBT)
description: The DBT emotion regulation module: how to identify emotions, reduce vulnerability to emotional mind, act opposite to urges, and check the facts.

title: Interpersonal Effectiveness (DEAR MAN)
description: DBT's DEAR MAN skills: Describe, Express, Assert, Reinforce, Mindful, Appear confident, Negotiate. The most practical script for assertive communication in relationships.

title: Understanding Panic Disorder
description: What happens in the brain and body during a panic attack, how panic disorder develops, and why the treatment (exposure, not avoidance) feels counterintuitive but works.

title: Self-Harm: Understanding Urges
description: A compassionate, non-judgmental guide to understanding self-harm urges — why they occur, what function they serve, and distress tolerance alternatives from DBT.

title: Motivational Interviewing Basics
description: How motivational interviewing resolves ambivalence about change — the stage of change model and strategies to move from contemplation to action.

title: The Mind-Gut Connection
description: 90% of serotonin is made in the gut. The bidirectional relationship between gut microbiome and mental health — and what you can eat to support your emotional wellbeing.

title: Dr Gabor Maté: Trauma and the Body
description: World-renowned trauma expert Dr Gabor Maté explains how childhood trauma shapes adult physiology, disease, and addiction — and why compassion, not willpower, heals.

title: EMDR Demonstration Session
description: Watch a live EMDR session demonstrating bilateral stimulation, desensitisation, and installation phases. Demystifies the therapy and shows what to expect.

title: Dr Julie Smith: Why Has Nobody Told Me This?
description: Clinical psychologist Dr Julie Smith's most popular videos — practical mental health skills for managing panic, low mood, motivation, and self-esteem. Clear, warm, non-clinical.

title: 5-Minute Mindfulness Bell Meditation
description: Set a recurring bell sound every 5 minutes during your day. At each bell, take one conscious breath. This micro-mindfulness practice has been shown to significantly reduce stress.

title: Worry Postponement Technique
description: Schedule a specific 15-minute daily worry time. When anxious thoughts arise outside this window, write them down and postpone them. Reduces total worry time by 35% in studies.

title: Morning Sunlight Protocol
description: Get 10 minutes of direct sunlight within 30-60 minutes of waking. Andrew Huberman's most recommended single intervention for sleep, mood, and energy — backed by photoreceptor research.

title: Cold Plunge for Mood Elevation
description: Deliberate cold exposure (3-8 minutes at 10-15°C) produces a 2.5× increase in dopamine and norepinephrine lasting 3+ hours. Protocol from Dr Susanna Søberg's research.

title: Radical Acceptance Practice (DBT)
description: Radically accepting reality as it is — not as you wish it were — is the pivotal DBT distress tolerance skill. Includes a step-by-step practice and the distinction between acceptance and approval.
